LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 TO PLEAD THE ALLEGED FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY AND TO PLEAD WHY THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATION GIVEN THE ISSUE DATE OF THE POLICY REFERRED TO IN THE COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4, 5, 9 AND 12 TO PLEAD THE NATURE OF THE CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS AND DEFENDANT AND THE SPECIFIC BREACHED TERM. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 7 TO SPECIFY RELIEF SOUGHT THAT DOES NOT MERELY ADDRESS PAST WRONGS. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6, 10 AND 11 TO PLEAD WHY THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY APPLICABLE STATUTES OF LIMITATION. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAWN VALENTINE OF THE BARGER & WOLEN LLP, TEL. NO. 743-3731 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT - DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 TO PLEAD THE ALLEGED FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY AND TO PLEAD WHY THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATION GIVEN THE ISSUE DATE OF THE POLICY REFERRED TO IN THE COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4, 5, 9 AND 12 TO PLEAD THE NATURE OF THE CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS AND DEFENDANT AND THE SPECIFIC BREACHED TERM. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 7 TO SPECIFY RELIEF SOUGHT THAT DOES NOT MERELY ADDRESS PAST WRONGS. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6, 10 AND 11 TO PLEAD WHY THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY APPLICABLE STATUTES OF LIMITATION. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
